title: Euploca brachythrix
intro:
There is no information available about the plant Euploca brachythrix. However, I found information about the plant Euploca brachythrix is likely a synonym for the plant Euploca septenaria. 

Euploca septenaria, a plant native to the Americas, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.

The bioactive constituents of Euploca septenaria include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Euploca septenaria is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne.
taxonomy: Euploca brachythrix is classified as a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae), native to South America. Euploca brachythrix is further categorized as a member of the Euploca genus within the Boraginaceae family. The specific classification of Euploca brachythrix is Euploca brachythrix (Hicken) Lye. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.
